full_prompt: Design one representative 5 m3 cylindrical ferrocement household water storage tank compliant with the CAWST Household Water Storage Tanks construction manual (2019). The tank is 2.0 m inner diameter, 1.6 m straight-wall height, with a flat (or slightly domed) ferrocement top and a ferrocement base slab, designed to store potable water at atmospheric pressure with a maximum fill head of 1.6 m from the base. Wall construction is ferrocement: 30 mm nominal wall thickness comprising three layers of 13 mm hexagonal chicken-wire mesh + two layers of 6 mm reinforcing bars at 150 mm each way (vertical + circumferential), embedded in a 1:3 cement:sand mortar of minimum compressive strength 25 MPa. The wall thickness schedule follows the CAWST tank manual: at any given depth from the free surface the hoop tensile stress induced in the cementitious composite by hydrostatic pressure must be checked against the tensile capacity of the ferrocement cross-section with a safety factor of at least 2 on hoop stress. For a 5 m3 tank (~2.0 m diameter, 1.6 m height, hydrostatic head up to 1.6 m giving gamma_w * h = 15.7 kPa at base) the maximum hoop force per unit height = p * r = 15.7 kPa * 1.0 m = 15.7 kN/m. The base slab is 80 mm ferrocement with equivalent reinforcement, supported on a prepared gravel pad. Inlet, outlet, overflow, and cleanout openings are locally reinforced. Target materials: mortar fc >= 25 MPa, steel mesh fy >= 250 MPa, rebar fy >= 420 MPa. Deliverable is a CAD assembly of the tank shell, base slab, top, and reinforcement representation, suitable for linear static structural FEA under hydrostatic self-weight + water load. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required; hydrostatic pressure is a static gravity load.
short_prompt:
Design a 5 m^3 cylindrical ferrocement household water-storage tank (two-layer 6 mm rebar grid + three layers 13 mm hexagonal chicken-wire mesh in 1:3 cement:sand mortar, flat or slightly domed top, ferrocement base slab on a gravel pad) per the CAWST Household Water Storage Tanks construction manual (2019). Mortar fc >= 25 MPa. Hoop-stress safety factor >= 2.0 against the tensile capacity of the ferrocement section.

Envelope: inner diameter 2.0 m, straight-wall height 1.6 m, ~30 mm wall, 80 mm base slab.

Load cases.
- LC1 hydrostatic full: 1.6 m water head, giving linear internal pressure up to ~15.7 kPa at the base.
- LC2 self-weight: gravity on the shell, top, and base.

prompt.title: 5 m3 ferrocement household water tank per CAWST
prompt.brief: Design a 2.0 m diameter x 1.6 m cylindrical ferrocement tank with 30 mm wall and SF 2 on hoop stress per CAWST manual.

boundary_conditions.base_slab_bottom: supported on gravel pad, vertical reaction only

objective.primary: pass SF 2 on hoop stress + capacity check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: CAWST wall-thickness-vs-head schedule for H <= 2.0 m

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static storage, no sloshing requirement at household scale.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Internal pressure keeps shell in tension; no buckling.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Seismic check when tank is above PGA 0.1 g
